info.title: MeasureX API
info.description: Construction takeoff and measurement platform API

get.summary: Get project with full hydrated state
get.description:
Returns the project with all classifications, polygons, scale, pages,
and thumbnail bundled in a single round-trip for client hydration.

sheetNames.description: Map of page number to extracted sheet name

400.description: No file provided or invalid format

404.description: No PDF uploaded for this project

get.summary: List all pages for a project

post.description: Creates a new measurement classification. Broadcasts `classification:created` via SSE.

Creates a measurement polygon. Area and linearFeet are auto-computed from points
if not provided. Broadcasts `polygon:created` via SSE.

Sets the drawing scale. This is critical for converting pixel measurements
to real-world units. Broadcasts `scale:updated` via SSE.

pixelsPerUnit.description: How many pixels equal one real-world unit

get.description:
Returns quantities recalculated from polygon geometry and current scale.
Units: SF for area, FT for linear, EA for count.

Generates a 3-sheet workbook: Summary, Quantities, and Assemblies.
Quantities are recalculated from geometry.

post.summary: Run AI takeoff on a PDF page
post.description:
Renders the specified PDF page to an image and sends it to the AI engine
for blueprint element detection. Requires a PDF to already be uploaded.

post.summary: Apply AI-detected elements to the project
post.description:
Creates classifications and polygons from AI detection results.
Deduplicates classifications by name (case-insensitive) and polygons
using 80% point overlap threshold.

Analyzes a base64-encoded blueprint image for construction elements
without requiring a project. Uses GPT-4o-mini.
Matches polygons by classificationId and area (within 20% tolerance).

projectId.description: Optional project ID to include project sheet results

post.summary: Search for elements in a blueprint image using vision AI

get.description:
Opens an SSE connection for real-time project updates. Despite the route
name, this is SSE (not WebSocket). Sends keepalive comments every 15s.
Buffers last 50 events per project for replay.

**Event types:** `connected`, `polygon:created`, `polygon:updated`,
`polygon:deleted`, `classification:created`, `classification:updated`,
`classification:deleted`, `scale:updated`, `assembly:created`,
`assembly:updated`, `assembly:deleted`

parameters.name: lastEventId
parameters.description: Resume from this event ID (also accepts Last-Event-Id header)
